headline: Standard Motor Products Releases 505 New Numbers in First Quarter
summary: Standard Motor Products, Inc. (SMP) is pleased to announce that it released more than 500 new part numbers in Q1 of 2026. This includes new applications from SMP's Vehicle Control and Temperature Control divisions for late-model import and domestic vehicles.

headline: GM Sales Fall Nearly 10% as the Auto Industry Sputters
summary: General Motors’  sales plunged in the first quarter of the year, falling 9.7%, in its biggest drop in almost four years, as high interest rates and vehicle prices across the industry held buyers back even before the impact of rising gas prices is fully felt.  “Abnormal is now normal,” GM North American chief  Duncan Aldred  said, speaking at an event ahead of the New York Auto Show.  Analysts have been expecting auto sales to be flat or down for 2026, citing still-high interest rates, the end of electric-vehicle tax credits and broader economic uncertainty.

Analysts have been expecting auto sales to be flat or down for 2026, citing still-high interest rates, the end of electric-vehicle tax credits and broader economic uncertainty.

headline: Home Depot sets sights on AI with CTO appointment

headline: 1 Unprofitable Stock to Target This Week and 2 We Avoid
summary: Unprofitable companies can burn through cash quickly, leaving investors exposed if they fail to turn things around. Without a clear path to profitability, these businesses risk running out of capital or relying on dilutive fundraising.

headline: Rivian R2 Takes 2026 Electric Green Car of the Year; Lexus, Jeep, Ford, and Bintelli Round Out Winners
summary: Green Car Journal has announced the second round of its 2026 Green Car Awards™, with the Rivian R2 taking 2026 Electric Green Car of the Year™. Presented since 2005 and widely recognized as the most important environmental awards in the automotive field, the Green Car Awards™ highlight models that stand out for their environmental performance, advanced technology, and leadership in moving toward a more sustainable driving future.

headline: Ford CEO Makes 295 Times Its Worker Pay
summary: The SEC requires companies to disclose the relationship between the CEO’s pay and the median annual compensation of the company’s workers. Ford (NYSE: F) CEO Jim Farley made $27,519,557 in 2025. The worker’s pay on that basis (less his) was $93,397 Farley’s 2024 comp was $24,861,866. Executive Chairman Bill Ford (William Clay Ford, Jr.), who ... Ford CEO Makes 295 Times Its Worker Pay

summary: NTSB holds Ford Motor Co. liable for two fatal collisions involving its BlueCruise driver assistance system.

headline: Is Ford (F) Offering Value After Recent Share Price Pullback?
summary: Wondering if Ford Motor at around US$11.54 is offering genuine value or just looks cheap on the surface? This article helps you put the current share price into context. Ford's stock has had a mixed run, with a 2.5% decline over the past week, an 18.1% decline over the last 30 days, but a 22.1% return over the past year. Recent coverage has focused on Ford's position as a major US automaker and its ongoing efforts in areas like vehicle electrification and software enabled services, which...

headline: Tiger Woods Says He Is 'Stepping Away' To Seek Treatment After DUI Rollover Crash: 'Understand The Seriousness Of The Situation'
summary: Golfing legend Tiger Woods seeks treatment after DUI arrest and crash. Affidavit shows he had hydrocodone pills, appeared impaired.
